title: Copyright and AI: Chris’s paper and a government report
content: Chris writes: Last week saw the publication of two papers relating to copyright and AI. The first was the hotly awaited UK government impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence. The second was a paper I was asked to write for Library Plus – the journal of the National Library of Korea – on copyright and libraries in the age of generative AI.
creator: cbowiemorrison
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Copyright Literacy: a small pause
content: You may have noticed we haven’t run a webinar for a few months due to various life events taking over in the early part of this year. However, work still continues at Copyright Literacy Towers and we have been working on the Copyright Anxiety Study and will be presenting at LILAC 2026 next week. It will be great to catch up with Amanda Wakaruk who will be joining us in Sheffield and we have convened a panel entitled “It’s not Legal Advice, but is it Information Literacy“. So watch this space, the web
creator: Jane Secker
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Copyright trolling survey
content:
In January, Tim Riley and Liesl Rowe gave a webinar on the Return of the Copyright Trolls, an exploration into the ongoing issue of copyright trolling. A highlight of the session was the lively discussion from participants about their own battles with copyright trolls and what they were doing to prevent it.

Tim and Liesl are keen to compile responses from other institutions to get a better sense of how copyright trolling is affecting the HE community. To start doing this, they have crafted a survey,
creator: Kathryn D
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Webinar 85: Postponed
content:
Friday The 13th Part II – the body count continues…

After our bad luck in February, we had been hoping to avoid the curse of Friday the 13th in March. But just 28 days later, disaster has struck again. Maybe we can blame paranormal activity, but sadly we have had to take the difficult decision to postpone March’s webinar as well. Perhaps the theme of the webinar – anxiety, specifically copyright anxiety in the further education sector – was the omen we should have taken note of.

We hope to resume normal service soon, hopefully before midsummer and definitely before Hallowe
creator: Kathryn D
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: POSTPONED – Webinar 84
content:
Unfortunately, we have had to postpone Webinar 84 – Launch of the Copyright Mentorship Scheme, on Friday 13th February 2026.

Despite our best intentions to share the love of copyright for Valentine’s Day, instead we have been struck by the curse of Friday the 13th.

We hope to reschedule the webinar as soon as possible, and will post the new date and time once it’s been arranged.

But whether you are celebrating Valentine’s, Galentine’s or Palentine’s Day, everyone at Copyright Literacy sends you their love.
creator: Kathryn D
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Webinar 84 – Launch of the Copyright Mentoring Scheme – POSTPONED
content:
Unfortunately, we have had to postpone this month’s webinar. Details of the new date will be published soon.

In a Valentine’s Day special, members of the ALT CoOL SIG will formally launch their new peer mentoring scheme.

We are aware that copyright is a tricky area, and so in the spirit of sharing the burden (or love) of copyright support we are creating a peer mentoring scheme. The scheme is aimed at those looking to offer their services as a mentor, or to be mentored.

Join Jane and Chris along with Liesl Rowe and Irene Baranco Garcia to find out more about how it will operate and to have the opportunity to sign up either as a mentee or a mentor.</
creator: Kathryn D
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Refresh of the copyright literacy strategy
content:
To mark the start of 2026 Chris and I had a day of planning for the year. We often used to do a annual review at the end of each year to list out the publications we had written, the conferences we had spoken at and the research we had been involved in

This year we started out by reviewing the Copyright Literacy Strategy that we put together in 2020. Although we never formally published this document, we did refer to it over the last few years to help maintain direction and moment. We realised during our planning meeting that the 5 year duration of the strategy had just expired! We took this as a great opportunity to review whether we had
creator: Jane Secker
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Webinar 83 – Return of the copyright trolls
content:
The copyright trolls are back in town!

In our latest webinar, Tim Riley (Aberdeen) and Liesl Rowe (Leeds Beckett University) will discuss the issue of copyright trolling: what it is; how best to prevent it; and strategies for dealing with it, all with reference to case studies from their own institutions.

This webinar is a return to the topic, which was discussed in Webinar 39 when we explored the practice of some photographers using agencies to aggressively assert their copyright and send letters alleging copyright infringement to universities. We have noted this practice has escalated and so in this closed webinar for the education community we will discuss the problems this caus
creator: Kathryn D
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Webinar 82: Big Fat Copyright Quiz 2025
content:
“Oh COOLSIG Quiz, Oh Coolsig quiz,

How baffling are your questions….”

The last COOLSIG webinar of 2025 is on Friday, December 19th at 11:00am. And as it Christmas, that can mean only one thing – it’s time for the now traditional Big Fat Copyright Quiz Of The Year!

Santa and his helpful elf will be welcoming COOLSIG’s resident question mistress, Kathryn, as she attempts to put together a selection of questions on the events of 2025, copyright and any other random stuff that has lodged in her brain.

After ICEPOPS, she promises that there won’t be any more questions about either o
creator: Kathryn D
sourceDescription: Decoding copyright and bringing you enlightenment

title: Webinar 81: Highights of Icepops 2025
content:
This coming Friday, 14th November is a chance to catch up with 5 speakers from September’s Icepops conference, which took place at the John Rylands Library at the University of Manchester. We have 4 guests joining us live and one delivering a recorded presentation. This is your chance to hear:

Doug McCarthy – ‘The Sunflower is Mine’ – Paradoxical Patterns in the Public Domain.
